profileRecord.recordDesc:
<@profile.key>CODENAME: Pogranichnik 
GENDER: Male 
AUTHENTICATION: Rhodes Island 
DOB: July 23 
RACE: Liberi 
[ORIPATHY INFECTION STATUS] 
Oripathy negative, based on medical examination reports. 

[INTEGRATED PHYSICAL EXAMINATION] 
PHYSIOLOGICAL STRENGTH: Excellent 
COMBAT SKILL: Excellent 
TACTICAL ACUMEN: Outstanding
ORIGINIUM ARTS ASSIMILATION: Standard</>

<@profile.key>Pogranichnik (ID name: Khorsyn) is a Rhodes Island liaison and a Banner Commander for the Order of Steel Oath. He spent years at the Polar Circle front in the north of Talos-II. Given the needs of the current situation, Pogranichnik joined Endfield Industries to provide relevant combat support. 

Pogranichnik uncompromising looks of a professional soldier left me quite an impression when I first met him. He acts with great decisiveness and alacrity, and never with doubt nor hesitation. Once he promises to take upon a task, no matter how small or severe, he would see it through to the best of his abilities. 
Although Pogranichnik displayed no obvious preferences for the Big Three of the Band Accord, he exhibited a special emotional connection to the Mat' Gorodov of the UWST. He would not mind talking to crew mates seeking shade in the city plate construction yards. There were even occasions when he met UWST crew mates confused about life or suffering through the pain of shattered dreams. Pogranichnik is exceptional at boosting morale, even for commoners faraway from the battlefield. He always found a way to instill confidence back into people's hearts. Oathkeepers and UWST crew mates who knew Pogranichnik often highlight his cold, sharp, yet endearing sense of humor. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

<@profile.key>DOCUMENT: Two Photographs
An old photograph with yellowing edges, featuring Pogranichnik standing amongst a diverse crew of various races. The group stood before a fortified defense works forged of steel and covered by snow. Two Oathkeeper scouts stood atop the defenses, gazing into the far distance with binoculars. BACKGROUND: Contours of a fort could be seen in the slightly blurred horizon, along with large caliber cannons pointing at the skies. CHARACTERS OF NOTE: A particular crew mate standing next to Pogranichnik wore an expression of pride that stood out from the others. The man pointed at the skies where a giant vessel floated above everything. This photograph does not show the entirety of the said vessel. 

A photograph of a great hall within a fort, taken from above. Soldiers of the Order of Steel Oath who stood in the middle of the hall raised their cups into the air at Pogranichnik who stood on the stage. UPPER LEFT: Progranichnik placing a fist on the massive map behind him. UPPER RIGHT: A long dining table. A silvery haired old man with a chiseled face, though the features were obscured as he faced the other way. All those seated at the table wore badges and sashes of banner commanders, and like the soldiers, they also raised their glasses. LOWER LEFT: Young recruits rubbing shoulders with each other. One of them played an accordion and those nearby seemed to be singing and cheering loudly with their mouths open. NOTE: The hall is filled with plenty of individuals in festive spirits, but Pogranichnik never showed the picture to anyone, and neither did he frame it. 

DOCUMENT: Three Recipes
Entrée Recipe C5: Primal Cut Burdenbeast Steak Set. Courtesy of UWST. A primal cut of burdenbeast shank, nutrient-fortified, smoked. Standard UWST Guards military fare. Winner of the United Workers' Syndicates of Talos II Central Production Planning Committee Awards for 20 consecutive years. 
"The Shieldguards are still getting used to the unique taste of the fortifying agents. Besides that, this is most impeccable as field rations. It offers sufficient calories, nutrition, and the fillet has been prepared to provide an excellent texture — I remember tasting something similar before, something from Ursus." 

Entrée Recipe C8: Metal Crab Doria Set. Courtesy of TGCC. La Fantoma metal crabs and Kjersch cheese come together in this preparation by master chefs of Neo Tsukushi. This is designed to create explosions of freshness that blend the Talosian hills and oceans. A gastronomic experience fitting for the indomitable Oathkeepers. 
"A culinary wonder rarely seen in self-cooking rations. They are quite effective in maintaining the morale of the fighting banners. However, caloric content of this particular item is currently incapable of meeting the Shieldguards' requirements. Please improve upon it." 

Entrée Recipe C12: Instant Shangshu Hot Pot. Courtesy of HAS. These words were messily scribbled on the back of the rations crate: "Khorsyn, your soldiers are battle-hardened veterans. And what do you feed them with? Trough slop so stale that they're crying FOWL! Since you've been most respectful to this tianshi, this entire crate is on me. Guranteed to dispel the cold and put heat in your guts. Don't forget to warm the burdo-tallow for the grease dip." 
"This entrée has utterly decimated my warriors. Half of the Shieldguards in the entire banner cried out for painkillers. A number of them managed to find strength to crawl on the ground and stuff handfuls of snow in their mouths. However, no one in the banner complained about the cold in the 30-klick foot march." 

DOCUMENT: A Map 
A massive map illustrated on massive piece of tanned cystybeast hide. It was made just in case all instruments and machinery running on Originium technology fail. 
The map is densely marked with Order of Steel Oath defense zones, supply routes, and key strong points. Arsenals of Fort Forgefane have been included as well. 
The southern area of the map shows dozens of roads meandering along the contours that connect the various Order forts. A few of these routes pass through Myrkwood — lifelines that connect the Civilization Band to the Order of Steel Oath and the very edge of the Northern Corridor. The northern area of the map shows a jagged battle line that stretches across the land like a scar. It stretches from the main defense zones of the Order and ends only at the fringe of the Aurora. 
In the memo section furnished next to the map display rack, Pogranichnik, the author of this map, left this handwritten message: 
1,237 names. That's the worth of this. </>

<@profile.key>DOCUMENT: Excerpt of a Conversation Recorded at Fort ████
"Sir Instructor, please wait! That lesson was most engaging. You must be a true expert in fighting the Aggeloi. We just heard those warriors calling you — Khorsyn?" 
"The lesson will serve its purpose if it helps you survive an actual engagement. Is there anything else I can assist you with?" 
"Oh, this is great! I'm Vadim. Vadim Ignatyevich Smirnov. This one here is Muravyov. We're both from the UW." 
"Ignaty? I know this name. Did you leave your home because of your father, recruit? He often showed me pictures of you." 
"Yes. When he was around, he often mentioned your heroic deeds in his letters. He was proud to fight along your side against the Aggeloi that tried to breach our defenses — Always. In every letter." 
"Yeah! When Vasha and I were kids, we were ... trying to be the heroes described in the letters! We played games pretending to be the fighting Shieldguards. Everyone wants to be as brave as you!" 
"We... Well, Muravyov and I passed the selection trials of the Fort. We want to inherit our father's courage, even at the cost of our lives." 
"A most respectable goal. But I want you to understand that sacrifice is never the objective. On the contrary, the Order fights to avoid that altogether." 
"Instructor Khorsyn, back when I was still at school, I've always been picturing this in my mind! The Aggeloi of the north smashing themselves into the frontlines and the banners buckling under their weight. Just when the buggers were about to breach, the Oathkeepers heard the mighty thud of iron shields slammed into the earth — Khorsyn and his Shieldguards had arrived and they charged forth! That mighty thud told everyone that you were the unbreakable front! The grounds where you stand would never be taken!" 
"Vasha is right, instructor. We're not afraid of sacrifice!" 
"No. I've seen too many aspirants falling at this place. If you really want to be a true Shieldguard, being a hero or sacrificing your lives are not the qualities you need." 
"But you..." 
"Son, you must learn to survive. Only then could you appear at places that need you most. Then you must place the shield before you, and —" 
"Help the others ... survive?" 
"Exactly. You actually listened during class. This is the most basic tactic of the Shieldguard wall. We stand side by side to shelter the others and help them live — We do not care if they're trying to save their skins, inheriting a legacy, or getting ready for an effective tactical counterstrike." 
"Your lessons is truly empowering, Instructor Khorsyn! I ... we ... AHEM! Vasha, say something!" 
"Yeah! I ... I mean, Muravyov and I will both become Shieldguards! We shall serve in your banner!" 
"Then I shall look forward to the day when I fight along your sides." </>

<@profile.key>DOCUMENT: Summary of the Battle of █████ by the TGCC United Services Research Center: 
Esteemed representatives attending this joint session of TGCC concerns and corporations, I am Tanner Orrigan, special observer and battle asset insurance agent. I am here to give you a report on the Battle of █████, which I witnessed and experienced as an observer embedded with the Order of Steel Oath. 
This high intensity conflict between the Order of Steel Oath and the Northern Aggeloid Host resulted in severe losses of military assets, contributed by all signatories of the Band Accord. Primary asset losses incurred by the Order include: 31 pieces of artillery walkers and other heavy UAVs for reinforcing a number of defenses zones. For details, please refer to the printed annex a3 provided next to you. 
...... 
Obviously, the sudden appearance of the unknown entity forced the Order to stop their offensive against the Aurora. But I would like to remind all esteemed representatives that all losses incurred in this offensive strongly prove our willingness to further our partnership and provision of unconditional aid to the Order. Once we assist the Order with the construction of industrial production facilities, the North will only need more goods from various companies. There is a healthy chance that the current model of bulk equipment purchases will give way to a new paradigm of a multi-tiered technology and intellectual property partnership, one that is far more beneficial to our interests. 
...... 
During this battle, I was embedded with a banner that was part of the main offensive. We approached a ridge and made our way towards a forward fort. However, unassisted vision was unable to pinpoint the exact location of the fort as the massive Aggeloid host blanketed the entire topography — This ended when the Banner Commander Khorsyn, the one who had been most uninhibited in expending military assets, began his attack on the other side of the Aggeloid host. The Shieldguards advanced in formation, pushed the battle lines over the ridge, and deployed signal flares at the high grounds to help the fort artillery to direct their firing. 
It was during this time that the unknown entity, the very one that blunted the other Banner offensives, re-appeared at the bottom of the Aurora. Esteemed representatives, I would like to take this moment to voice a consensus that I have reached with the Order of Steel Oath: Under no circumstances can we allow any entity of its type to enter densely populated areas. So, back to the topic. When the entity re-appeared, I was in the relative safety of a forward command center. However, Æther activities triggered by the said entity had rendered my recording devices inoperable. At this moment of desperation, Khorsyn led his Banner of Shieldguards and attacked from the very front. The Shieldguards raised their shields and pushed the advance with steadfast determination and valiance. I am afraid I could not describe what I saw next in an objective manner. I saw red flashes hewing apart the greatest Aggeloi, turning them into silent headstones. Every hole that appeared in the bastion of iron shields was quickly sealed by another Shieldguard. After 47 minutes of brutal battle, Khorsyn and his Shieldguards managed to carve a path right through the Aggeloid host and besieged the unknown entity that was responsible for killing hundreds of the Oathkeepers' best soldiers. In a single exchange, Khorsyn split the statuesque carapace right through the median plane. The strange matter encased within the unknown entity attempted escape by ejecting itself into the air. However, fire rained down from the skies as steam vented from the gaps in the armor. The final throes of resistance were extinguished at long last. 
...... 
Esteemed representatives, the Order of Steel Oath has proved their indispensability through this battle. Our support is more than just supporting the spirit of altruism espoused by the Band Accords. It is an investment towards the crucial strategic resilience of the Northern Front. Another objective of this report is to clarify that the actual legacy of the Order lies not within their Vault of Relics, but amongst key personnel, like Khorsyn. The values of these individuals cannot be appraised, and neither could they be traded. However, "Heroes of the Band" will be a most valuable investment opportunity for the TGCC — That is without question. </>

<@profile.key>Mat' Gorodov. 
I've seen this name countless times in UWST art films before my actual visit. She is the heart of the United Workers' Syndicates of Talos II, replete with massive command towers that scraped the skies and integrated city plate construction yards. She resembled the nomadic cities that I've seen in my memories — Solid and absolutely gargantuan. 
But when I entered the city, the first thing I saw was not awe-inspiring industrial mega structures. No, it was crew mates carrying building materials and rushing towards their work stations. The crews never seemed exhausted, and they labored to transport the lifeblood of this city to very corner. Every inch of concrete bore the prints of their hands. 
I did not seek the service of a local guide as I intended to satisfy my curiosities myself. Instead, I chose to shadow a crew mate who just concluded his day of labor, and followed him into a massive, minimalist building. After a bit of jostling, I arrived at the second floor of the main hall and sat myself down on a soft chair — And I only realized, at that moment, that I was in a crew theater. The crew mate who just finished his shift had already changed into his costume and became an opera performer. 
I did not succeed in guessing the plot of the performance. They did not sing praises for the heroes of labor. Instead, it was a comedy that critiqued bureaucracy — The crew of workers tacitly worked together and "misled" the corrupt official into discovering Pure Gold, something that does not exist on Talos-II. In the end, the official ate the bitter fruits of his greed. Of course, the workers remained the heroes of this tale. But these heroes were humorous and shared a boundless passion for a happy life. 
The show ended and I left the theater with various thoughts weighing heavily in my mind. It was then when a girl around ten years of age and her twin brother realized who I was. "An Oathkeeper! From the Order of the Steel Oath!" they squealed excitedly. The children stood in front of me and saluted me in the way of the Order. Their parents and other workers pulled them away with some difficulty, as they had an endless stream of questions that I find rather difficult to answer. But I could tell, from their expressions, that these adults would have done the same as the children if they had been younger. 
I had a dream before I left Rhodes Island. I dreamed of a banquet. The children of army group nobility were dressed lavishly and kept under the watch of servants and governesses, like intricate dolls meticulously prepared for show. These groomed children exuded the same innocence and passion given off by the twins. But the gazes of these young nobles also carried something else. Was it family honor? Or a desire for power wielded by their elders? I could not tell, but I do know that the taint did not come from their own accord. I witnessed the taint transforming into ambition and shone with the cold glint of schemes and conspiracies. In the end, they became something that I could no longer recognize. 
After I left the theater, I trailed the streets of Mat' Gorodov on my own. It was then when a strange sense of freedom caught up to me. I could still recall elite imperial strikers following me in those great charges, the girl with Oripathy, underground clinics reduced to ruin, crowds trying to make a living, and children playing with each other. These memories lingered somewhere within me, but they no longer weighed me down. 
A new milestone has been erected upon the grave of days long gone. I concluded my short tour of the city and quickly returned north. Therein lies a battlefield where an entire generation fell. A place absent the dust of the old world nor grand blueprints for the new. A field of carnage where survival was the only objective. 
That is where we fight. 
 — Audio transcript recorded by an anonymous individual, currently archived at Rhodes Island</>

profileVoice.voiceDesc: In position and standing by.
Ready to break camp on command.
A soldier is born to fight.
Survival is never free. We always have to pay.
One must learn to wield violence — not for good nor malice, but for the sake of survival.
To a quartermaster, proper armament is the first step toward victory.
Remissness and negligence pose the deadliest threat on the battlefield.
A valuable battle ... My fighting spirit has been stirred.
No badge or ceremony is needed. I fully appreciate the significance of this... Thank you for your trust, Endministrator of Endfield Industries.
If this happened at the Oathkeepers' fortress, I'd have to play the host at tonight's feast.
Whether on the battlefield or elsewhere, this is an honor. Thank you for your trust, Endministrator. I'll prove that this trust is well placed through my actions on the frontlines.

War remains a brutal affair on Talos-II. Numerous souls have marched into the north, fighting and falling so that the rest of this world may live. Endmin, I have no doubt that your actions will put a solemn end to this cruel cycle of war and sacrifice.

Pogranichnik, Rhodes Island operator, and officer serving the Order of Steel Oath, reporting for duty. The situation is urgent. I respectfully request a brief on your battle plan, at your earliest convenience.
Let's go over the battle plan once more, shall we?
The slightest slip of mind decides who lives ... and who dies in battle.
I've never tried this sort of work... But from a strategic perspective, it may be worth a try.
Greetings, Endministrator. Here for another inspection tour?
Salutations, Endministrator.

Dijiang is much larger than I expected, but I hardly spent any time familiarizing myself with its entire layout. That's thanks to the ingenious and impeccable designs by its creator. It's a great starting point for a great dream, Endmin, and I'm eager to see how that dream unfolds.

The Automated Industry Complex is far beyond what I imagined. With the Protocol Automation-Core, the suite of Terran military-industrial machines will finally have a potent heart, one that sustains stable deliveries of essential resources to every imperiled corner of the planet. Endmin, your work will save countless lives. On that I give you my sincere promise.

The Arsenal Engineers of Endfield are truly impressive. They managed to complete weapon maintenance for every personnel despite the tight schedules... Such efficiency is rare, even on the Northern Front. Though I must admit, I didn't expect someone that young to be in charge of your Arsenal.

I've reviewed Instructor Lil' Dodge's training program for you. The content is practical and the difficulty curve is well-balanced. I don't intend to make any changes to it, though I added one or two extra minor trials based on my experience... But I trust that won't be a problem for you, Endministrator.

Before you were awakened, I hadn't taken part directly in Endfield operations for years. To me, whether I serve at Rhodes Island, Endfield, or the Order of Steel Oath makes no difference. I only want to protect what I believe is worth protecting. And this time, I shall advance, and never retreat.

Every time I look out over Talos-II, I feel a surge of inspiration. Massive swarms of abominations, capable of engulfing the entire planet, are forced into cauldrons in the auroral north. Mere soldiers of flesh and blood were the brick and mortar that held the enemy in check. This is one of the Oathkeeper's greatest military feats, an undeniable proof that we are worthy ... of the immense responsibility of preserving civilization.
I stumbled upon this in the north... It might prove useful to you, Endministrator.
Thank you for your gift, Endministrator. I take it as a commendation as well... And please accept this as my token of gratitude.

After leaving Rhodes Island, I first visited the Industrial Mothership of the UWST. For some reason, I wanted to see what fruits a certain chapter of history had borne in this land. When I laid eyes on that indomitable city, I was deeply moved. If the children of Ursus can leave the past behind, start things anew, and achieve their own feats of honor and glory, then I, Khorsyn, have no reason to hesitate any longer.

Military life with the Oathkeepers has taught me three lessons: First, trust your comrades; second, never back down easily; third, never push your luck. In the north, violating any of these principles could bring misfortune to you or your comrades.

During my time with the Oathkeepers, I reorganized and trained the Shieldguards. The idea took shape on the night before I reached the Oathkeepers' bastion complex. Massive structures rose in the shadow of the mountains. Sami's Myrkwood stretched out in the darkness, as if dark green flames creeping along the riverbanks. Countless tombstones stood in rows, forming a wall that held back the enemies from the north. The Oathkeepers is a shield protecting the Civilization Band, and I've forged another shield for them. I believe these soldiers will never break.

Hellagur is more like a mentor and friend. I've come to understand his regrets and guilt, and I've learned from his thoughts and skills. There is no Hellagur the Ursine General here — only Khorsyn of Rhodes Island and Endfield. At times, I feel grateful I have no past to weigh me down. Other times, I feel ... the ache of solitude. If what I'm protecting turns out to be another empty ideal, then it's no different from the hollow glory of the Ursus Empire. Endministrator, I hope that ... you'll be one to give me an answer.

Endmin, it is my solemn wish that your vision is fulfilled; that everyone at Rhodes Island can return to a life of peace and common joys; that the Oathkeepers can finally beat their swords to plowshares and enjoy the company of their loved ones; and that life can finally sink its roots deep into this ancient planet. I look forward to celebrating every victory with you and your friends. Even in the face of a devastating defeat, I pray that you will survive by any means necessary. If you do, then everything I've done will be worth it.

The youths of Op Team Z7 are truly impressive. They've only received basic military training, but managed to develop combat awareness on par with that of standard military combatants. Such achievement is rare, and commendable.

I worked all kinds of jobs in the north. Besides being a commander, I also doubled as a quartermaster and drill instructor. I forged weapons, milked new breeds of cystybeasts, and even made grape wine...

What is a soldier's honor? I'm afraid even I cannot give a definitive answer. But should you ever visit the Oathkeepers' fortress in the north, I urge you to visit our memorial... Every name engraved upon the monument once held an answer to that question.

The Grand Treasurer manages a massive vault of legacies. They include not only relics of Oathkeeper legends, but also currently unrestorable Terra technologies, and mysterious artifacts of the Precursors. I was once given the chance to choose a relic to wield, but I refused. I had already drawn on the glory of a past legend. For the rest of my life, I will leave behind my own glorious legacy.

Most of the time, Oathkeepers are fighting Aggeloi, Ankhors, and the disasters caused by Ætherside activities. But there have been extremely rare occasions where we faced inexplicable phenomena: traces of giant creatures, witness reports of peculiar individuals... This is why I immediately requested to return to Endfield when I heard the Endmin had been awakened.

The north is actually quite different from what most people imagine. After a century of development, the Oathkeepers' fortresses have become bustling hubs of commerce and entertainment. People became accustomed to constant war, and have created a rather vibrant way of life. Outside the fortresses, the Samifjod built settlements in Myrkwood to preserve their ancient knowledge and culture. Their caravans would often travel between the fortresses and the forest. Endmin, you should pay the north a visit. It's a beautiful place, and I'll gladly accompany you if that is your wish.

Industrial Mothership, the largest city of the UWST, now goes by many names and aliases. But I know her name was Simonch when she was still in Terra. She sank in a lake under the tyrannical rule of the Ursus Empire. I still don't know why the Empire salvaged her afterwards, nor why they repurposed that ruined city into an exclave in a foreign world. Nevertheless, she is now the largest nomadic city on Talos-II, and a spiritual symbol for many.

When I first chose my Shieldguards, I saw a motley of trainees even younger than me. My first task was to strip away their aggression, for they are guards and not attackers. Next, I taught them every tactic I knew so they not only defend, but become the living frontline itself. Finally, I made sure that they mastered survival... But in that regard, I've always felt that I haven't done enough. Sacrifice is never the objective. It is a last resort for the sole purpose of avoiding more sacrifices.

I've heard stories about Miss Ember, a most remarkable warrior. Her assignment to the south wasn't just about rebuilding her squad. Her commander wanted to give her a chance to lead a normal life. I believe you have noticed this already, Endmin. Sadly, she is unlikely to appreciate the meaning of this generosity. A soldier who could make peace with their past would never have gone so far as she has.

Contrary to what people say, the Samifjod are not xenophobes. It is true that for more than a century, they've been living under the lingering protection left behind by their All-Father. Yet, they generously offer help to travelers who respect Sami traditions. The Cyclops once gifted Sami's Teeth to the Seš'qamam to power the floating bastion. The Snowpriests are also more than willing to share their Arts with the Oathkeepers. It is clear that the Samifjod have found their own way of embracing a new life on Talos-II while preserving their heritage.
You might find that useful.
Keep an eye on that area, if you don't want to miss key supplies.
Maintain formation, especially in uncharted territories.
Stay alert, the enemy there is strong.
This'll shape up nicely — perfect for both offense and defense.
When we pool our strength, no task is beyond us.
Strategic reserves like these can shift the tide of battle.
Its strategic significance probably exceeds its operational value.
Adverse variables are best taken out during the earliest phases of war.
Every chance of recuperation counts. It might be your only chance to prepare for the next battle.
Hang in there. No rush.
That was an oversight.
All units! Incoming!
This is far from desperate.
This is no place ... for my war to end...
A brilliant strategy.
A textbook maneuver.
You flatter me.
We have proven the power of unity.
War is never about mercy.
Engage.
Our battle plan proved its worth.
The results speak for themselves.
It is worth a celebration.
Every victory bears its price... Hold dear what you've learned, but don't forget what we've paid.
Don't let failure weigh you down. Own it, and let it propel you to greater heights.
Move aside!
Pathetic!
Never tempt fate!
Never fear!
Never retreat!
At your command.
They're mine to end.
Crescent moon!
Half moon!
Full moon!
Rest in silence.
Your death awaits.
AT MY COMMAND!
Soldiers, ADVANCE!
Shieldguards! FORMATION!

chr_0029_pograni_ComboSkill.desc:
COMBO TRIGGER: When <#ba.crush>Crush</> or <#ba.fracture>Breach</> <#ba.consume>consumes</> the enemy's <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stack(s). 
Performs a number of slash sequences (up to 3) based on the max number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks <#ba.consume>consumed</>, dealing <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and recovering some SP. Each slash deals more DMG and gives better SP recovery. 
<@ba.info>Consuming 4 stacks of Vulnerability enhances the 3rd slash sequence.</>
chr_0029_pograni_ComboSkill.name: Full Moon Slash

chr_0029_pograni_NormalAttack.desc:
BASIC ATTACK: 
An attack with up to 5 sequences that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. As the controlled operator, <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> also deals <@ba.poise>{poise:0}</> Stagger. 

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher attack that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and recovers some SP.
chr_0029_pograni_NormalAttack.name: All-Out Offensive

chr_0029_pograni_NormalSkill.desc: Pogranichnik performs a 2-sequence slash attack at enemies in front of him, dealing <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, applying <#ba.fracture>Breach</>, and recovering SP based on the number of Vulnerability stacks <#ba.consume>consumed</>. The SP recovery effect can only trigger 1 time, even if the skill hits multiple targets.
chr_0029_pograni_NormalSkill.name: The Pulverizing Front

chr_0029_pograni_UltimateSkill.desc: Summons 4 Shieldguards to advance and push all enemies along the way towards the target, dealing <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> while generating 5 points of <@ba.key>Steel Oath</>. After an enemy suffers a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or takes DMG from Pogranichnik's combo skill, Pogranichnik consumes 1 point of <@ba.key>Steel Oath</> and summons 1 Shieldguard to harass the said enemy, dealing <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and recovering some SP. When the last <@ba.key>Steel Oath</> point is consumed, all 4 Shieldguards will initiate a Decisive Assault that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to the target while recovering plenty of SP.
chr_0029_pograni_UltimateSkill.name: Shieldguard Banner, Forward

chr_0029_pograni_talent_2_2.desc:
Ultimate <@ba.key>Shieldguard Banner, Forward</> improved: Any operator triggering the ultimate's subsequent effects also gains <@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> for <@ba.vup>{duration:0}</>s. 
<@ba.info>Requires activation of talent: The Living Banner.</>

chr_0029_pograni_potential_1.desc: Battle skill <@ba.key>The Pulverizing Front</> improved: When hitting at least 2 enemies, <@ba.vup>{atb_return:0}</> SP is <#ba.return>returned</>.

chr_0029_pograni_potential_4.desc: Ultimate <@ba.key>Shieldguard Banner, Forward</> improved: Ultimate Energy cost <@ba.vup>-{1-costvalue:0%}</>.

chr_0029_pograni_talent_2_1.desc:
Ultimate <@ba.key>Shieldguard Banner, Forward</> improved: Any operator triggering the ultimate's subsequent effects also gains <@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> for <@ba.vup>{duration:0}</>s. 
<@ba.info>Requires activation of talent: The Living Banner.</>

chr_0029_pograni_potential_5.desc: Combo skill <@ba.key>Full Moon Slash</> improved: Length of cooldown <@ba.vup>{coolDown:0}</>s; SP recovery is increased to <@ba.vup>{atb_ratio:0.0}</> times the original.

chr_0029_pograni_talent_1_1.desc:
During battle, recovering <@ba.vup>{atb_gain:0}</> SP with his own skills grants <@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> for <@ba.vup>{duration:0}</>s. 
<@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> effects: ATK <@ba.vup>+{atk_up:0%}</> and Arts Intensity <@ba.vup>+{physpell_up:0}</>. 
<@ba.info>This effect can reach 3 stacks. Duration of each stack is counted separately.</>

chr_0029_pograni_talent_1_2.desc:
During battle, recovering <@ba.vup>{atb_gain:0}</> SP with his own skills grants <@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> for <@ba.vup>{duration:0}</>s. 
<@ba.key>Fervent Morale</> effects: ATK <@ba.vup>+{atk_up:0%}</> and Arts Intensity <@ba.vup>+{physpell_up:0}</>. 
<@ba.info>This effect can reach 3 stacks. Duration of each stack is counted separately.</>

spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_1_1.desc: Assign to Manufacturing Cabin to grant weapon EXP material production efficiency +20%
spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_1_1.name: Hone the Weapons β

spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_1_2.desc: Assign to Manufacturing Cabin to grant weapon EXP material production efficiency +30%
spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_1_2.name: Hone the Weapons γ

spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_2_1.desc: Assign to Manufacturing Cabin to slow Mood Drop of all operators in it by 14%
spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_2_1.name: Morale Boost β

spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_2_2.desc: Assign to Manufacturing Cabin to slow Mood Drop of all operators in it by 18%
spaceship_skill_chr_0029_pograni_2_2.name: Morale Boost γ

tag_expert_tactic.desc:
Battlefield Tactics: Military Theory
He believes that every theory should be traceable to actual wars, and applicable to real battles.

tag_hobby_culture.desc:
Culture: Living History Book
His preferred approach to learning about this world is to study its history and everything that happened to this land.

tag_expert_team.desc:
Team Management: Mentor
To the young recruits, he always attempts to share his experience and knowledge, and impress upon them the ideals and aspirations that he has held dear for all this time.

tag_hobby_knowledge.desc:
Mind Honing: Chess Master
"It's less of a match and more like a hard-fought battle." — Every operator who played chess with Pogranichnik felt they could relate to this feeling.

item_pic_1_chr_0029_pograni.name: Queenside Castling
item_pic_1_chr_0029_pograni.author: FE
item_pic_1_chr_0029_pograni.description: Getting this unlocks Pogranichnik's snapshot: "Queenside Castling". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_1_chr_0029_pograni.decoDescription: He always appears where he is needed most.

item_pic_3_chr_0029_pograni.name: The King and the Knight
item_pic_3_chr_0029_pograni.author: Kala
item_pic_3_chr_0029_pograni.description: Getting this unlocks Pogranichnik's snapshot: "The King and the Knight". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_3_chr_0029_pograni.decoDescription: But he bore absolutely no hatred for the Empire. More than a century had passed since the "Emperor" died, yet a new formation of shieldguards rose behind him.

item_pic_5_chr_0029_pograni.name: Checkmate
item_pic_5_chr_0029_pograni.author: 大明
item_pic_5_chr_0029_pograni.description: Getting this unlocks Pogranichnik's snapshot: "Checkmate". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_5_chr_0029_pograni.decoDescription: The smoke of ruin still lingered. Numerous pieces, living and dead, fell off the ponderous corpse. His blade served as the support of his body, his steel oath, and his vigil — And then he made his move. Checkmate.

0.desc: Guards are skilled at making enemies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</> and applying <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Statuses</>. They are also good damage dealers.
0.name: Guard

2.desc: Defenders are extremely tough. They are skilled in protecting and treating their teammates, as well as counterattacking the enemy.
2.name: Defender

4.desc: Supporters are skilled in applying various control effects that weaken the enemy. They can also buff and support their teammates.
4.name: Supporter

5.desc: Casters are skilled at applying <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Inflictions</> and <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reactions</>. They are also good damage dealers.
5.name: Caster

7.desc: Great at recovering Skill Points (SP) to help their teammates' skill casting.
7.name: Vanguard

8.desc: Strikers exploit Physical or Arts effects inflicted by other operators to deal decisive damage.
8.name: Striker

tag_power_syndicates.tagName: United Workers' Syndicates of Talos II

adv.electricFence.desc: An active high voltage fence is impassable. Follow the cable, find the switch, and shut down the fence for safe passage.
adv.electricFence.name: High Voltage Fence

fac.hub.desc: The Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) is the central facility of the Automated Industry Complex (AIC) Factory. Storage management becomes available when standing in the Core AIC Area of the PAC.
fac.hub.name: Protocol Automation-Core

adv.eswitch.desc: A reactive device changes its operation mode when attacked or hit by an explosion. It is often used to switch off <#adv.electricFence>High Voltage Fences</> or activate <#adv.jumpMachine>Bounce Devices</>.
adv.eswitch.name: Reactive Device

ba.spellvul.desc: Arts Susceptible targets temporarily take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.spellvul.name: Arts Susceptibility

item.quartzGlass.desc:
A fiber produced by refining <#item.quartzSand>Amethyst Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.quartzGlass.name: Amethyst Fiber

adv.jumpMachine.desc:
The controlled operator can use Bounce Devices to jump across a longer distance or reach high places unreachable with normal jumping.
Simply stand on a powered Bounce Device, then jump to use it.
adv.jumpMachine.name: Bounce Device

adv.powerTerminal.desc: An electric nexus is usually placed at the end of a power transmission line. Connect it to the power grid to supply power to nearby facilities. Electric Nexuses are often used to power gates and switches. Supply power to Electric Nexuses with Relay Towers. This then grants control access to gates or switches linked to them.
adv.powerTerminal.name: Electric Nexus

ba.crystenhance.desc: Cryo Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.crystenhance.name: Cryo Amp

ba.airborne.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
ba.airborne.name: Physical Status: Lift

item.bbflowerPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.bbflowerPowder.name: Aketine Powder

ba.burning.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.burning>Combustion</> takes <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> over time.
<@ba.burning>Combustion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>
ba.burning.name: Arts Reaction: Combustion

ba.crush.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks and deal massive <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>.
ba.crush.name: Physical Status: Crush

ba.burningonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
ba.burningonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Combustion

ba.enhance.desc: Amped targets deal more DMG of the element it is Amped for. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.enhance.name: Amp

item.bbflowerSeed.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Aketine Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Aketine.
Alternatively, Aketine Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
item.bbflowerSeed.name: Aketine Seed

ba.combo.desc: Next battle skill or ultimate cast by the team consumes the Link buff and deals more DMG, with battle skills getting a higher increase. Effects of the same type can stack, but with diminishing returns.
ba.combo.name: Link

item.procBattery1.desc:
A battery produced by packaging <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
item.procBattery1.name: LC Valley Battery

ba.conduct.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.conduct>Electrification</> takes more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>.
<@ba.conduct>Electrification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>
ba.conduct.name: Arts Reaction: Electrification

ba.conductonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
ba.conductonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Electrification

ba.physicalstatus.desc: Physical Statuses refer to physical debuffs applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.airborne>Lifted</>, <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</>, <#ba.crush>Crushed</>, and <#ba.fracture>Breached</>.
ba.physicalstatus.name: Physical Status

ba.consume.desc: Various Physical and Arts debuffs can be ended early. For example, <#ba.crush>Crush</> consumes <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> debuffs, while <#ba.burning>Combustion</> consumes <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> debuffs.
ba.consume.name: Debuff Consumption

ba.poiseknot.desc: Some powerful enemies have <@ba.poise>Stagger Nodes</>. Deal enough <@ba.poise>Stagger</> to reach the <@ba.poise>Stagger Node</> to interrupt such enemies and make them stumble.
ba.poiseknot.name: Stagger Node

ba.statuslevel.desc: Status Level is the number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> stacks (max 4) <#ba.consume>consumed</> when triggering a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>. The higher the <#ba.statuslevel>Status Level</>, the stronger the resulting <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>.
ba.statuslevel.name: Status Level

ba.corrupt.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> gradually loses resistance against all elements. 
<@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>
ba.corrupt.name: Arts Reaction: Corrosion

item.plantMossSeed1.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Buckflower Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Buckflowers. Alternatively, Buckflower Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
item.plantMossSeed1.name: Buckflower Seed

ba.corruptonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
ba.corruptonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Corrosion

ba.crystbreak.desc: To an enemy with active <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> effects will <#ba.consume>consume</> the <#ba.frozen>Solidification</> to trigger Shatter that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>.
ba.crystbreak.name: Shatter

ba.fireenhance.desc: Heat Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.fireenhance.name: Heat Amp

ba.rossi.desc: Rossi's battle skill applies a special status on the enemy. The affected target will take <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> over time, and suffers higher Physical DMG Taken and Heat DMG Taken.
ba.rossi.name: Razor Clawmark

ba.speedup.desc: Temporarily raises the movement speed of a target. The number represents the degree of the speed increase. Effects of the same type can stack until hitting the speed cap.
ba.speedup.name: Haste

ba.crystburst.desc: <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> + <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.cryst>Cryo Burst</> that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to enemies.
ba.crystburst.name: Arts Burst: Cryo

ba.crystinflict.desc:
<@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> = <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>
ba.crystinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Cryo

item.plantMoss3.desc:
A plant that is characterized by rough, gritty leaves. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</>.
item.plantMoss3.name: Sandleaf

ba.crystonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
ba.crystonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Cryo Infliction

ba.spelldmg.desc:
Damage can be divided into <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, <@ba.key>Arts DMG</>, and <@ba.ether>Æther DMG</>.
<@ba.key>Arts DMG</> can be further divided into <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>, <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>, <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>, and <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>.
ba.spelldmg.name: DMG Type

ba.crystvul.desc: Cryo Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.crystvul.name: Cryo Susceptibility

ba.dispel.desc: This removes specific debuffs from the operator.
ba.dispel.name: Dispel

item.ironPowder.desc: A powder produced by shredding <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironPowder.name: Ferrium Powder

ba.dot.desc: DMG over time cannot trigger critical hits.
ba.dot.name: DMG Over Time

item.bottledRecHp1.desc:
A capsule made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or filling <#item.plantMossPowder1>Buckflower Powder</> into an <#item.glassBottle>Amethyst Bottle</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
item.bottledRecHp1.name: Buck Capsule [C]

ba.fireburst.desc: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> + <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.fire>Heat Burst</> that deals <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> to enemies.
ba.fireburst.name: Arts Burst: Heat

ba.fireinflict.desc:
<@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> = <#ba.burning>Combustion</>
ba.fireinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Heat

ba.fireonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
ba.fireonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Heat Infliction

ba.firevul.desc: Heat Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.firevul.name: Heat Susceptibility

ba.fracture.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks, deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>, and temporarily increase the <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> taken by the enemy.
ba.fracture.name: Physical Status: Breach

ba.pulseonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
ba.pulseonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Electric Infliction

item.plantSpSeed1.desc:
Seeds produced by seed-picking <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</> plants with AIC Factory facilities.
Take Reed Rye Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>, then go to the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen to craft them into <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
item.plantSpSeed1.name: Reed Rye Seed

sys.manualCraftField.desc: <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> lets you craft planting plots with plant seeds and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>. Planting plots can be placed and grown at the Eco-Farm.
sys.manualCraftField.name: Planting

ba.frozen.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.frozen>Solidification</> cannot move, and applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> to the enemy triggers the <#ba.crystbreak>Shatter</> effect. 
<@ba.frozen>Solidification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>
ba.frozen.name: Arts Reaction: Solidification

item.plantMossSpc1.desc:
The Firebuckle is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its exceptionally brilliant flowers.
There is a chance of getting Firebuckles when harvesting <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> in the wilderness.
They can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
item.plantMossSpc1.name: Firebuckle

ba.frozenonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
ba.frozenonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Solidification

item.plantMossPowder1.desc:
A powder made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or shredding <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
item.plantMossPowder1.name: Buckflower Powder

ba.guard.desc: Protected targets temporarily take reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
ba.guard.name: Protect

ba.knockdown.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
ba.knockdown.name: Physical Status: Knock Down

ba.lastcombo.desc: Final Strike is the last sequence of basic attack. When the controlled operator's Final Strike hits the enemy, it also deals <@ba.poise>Stagger</> and restores some Skill Points (SP).
ba.lastcombo.name: Final Strike

ba.naturalburst.desc: <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> + <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.natur>Nature Burst</> that deals <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> to enemies.
ba.naturalburst.name: Arts Burst: Nature

ba.naturalenhance.desc: Nature Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.naturalenhance.name: Nature Amp

item.plantMossSeed3.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Sandleaf Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Sandleaves.
Alternatively, Sandleaf Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
item.plantMossSeed3.name: Sandleaf Seed

item.portSoilBbflower.desc:
Aketine Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.bbflower>Aketine</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.bbflowerSeed>Aketine Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Aketine.
item.portSoilBbflower.name: Aketine Plot

ba.naturalinflict.desc:
<@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> = <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>
ba.naturalinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Nature

ba.spellstatus.desc: <@ba.key>Arts Reactions</> are Arts debuffs that can be applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.burning>Combustion</>, <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>, <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, and <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>.
ba.spellstatus.name: Arts Reaction

sys.manualCraftDish.desc:
Fine Food & Meds (Fine F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>. 
Unlike <#sys.manualCraftTonic>Emergency Food & Meds</>, they can only be made using <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</>.
sys.manualCraftDish.name: Fine Food & Meds

ba.naturalonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
ba.naturalonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Nature Infliction

ba.naturalvul.desc: Nature Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.naturalvul.name: Nature Susceptibility

ba.pulseinflict.desc:
<@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> = <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>
ba.pulseinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Electric

ba.noguard.desc:
Hitting an enemy for the first time with a skill with <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> applies <@ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> instead of the actual Physical Status.
A <@ba.phy>Vulnerable</> enemy can be <#ba.airborne>Lifted</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</> to add more Vulnerability stacks (up to 4). Hitting a Vulnerable enemy with <#ba.crush>Crush</> or <#ba.fracture>Breach</> will <#ba.consume>consume</> the Vulnerability stacks.
ba.noguard.name: Vulnerable

sys.manualCraft.desc:
Open the Backpack panel to access the Crafting screen. Different raw materials can be crafted into useful items.
For example, <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</> can be crafted into <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Powder</> for restoring HP.
sys.manualCraft.name: Crafting

ba.originium.desc: The Endministrator's combo skill attaches Originium Crystals to enemies and immobilizes them.
ba.originium.name: Originium Crystal

ba.physicalenhance.desc: Physical Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.physicalenhance.name: Physical Amp

ba.physicalvul.desc: Physical Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.physicalvul.name: Physical Susceptibility

item.glassBottle.desc:
A bottle produced by moulding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.glassBottle.name: Amethyst Bottle

ba.pulseburst.desc: <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> + <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.pulse>Electric Burst</> that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> to enemies.
ba.pulseburst.name: Arts Burst: Electric

ba.slow.desc: Slowed targets temporarily suffer movement speed reduction. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
ba.slow.name: Slow

ba.pulseenhance.desc: Electric Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.pulseenhance.name: Electric Amp

item.originiumEnrPowder.desc:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.originiumEnrPowder.name: Dense Originium Powder

ba.pulsevul.desc: Electric Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.pulsevul.name: Electric Susceptibility

item.glassCmpt.desc:
A part produced by fitting <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.glassCmpt.name: Amethyst Part

ba.return.desc:
After spending SP and meeting certain criteria, some of the SP spent will be returned. 
SP Return is not "SP Recovery" and does not activate SP Recovery-triggered effects. Spending returned SP also does not give Ultimate Energy.
ba.return.name: SP Return

ba.shield.desc: The shield can absorb damage while it is active. Some shields may only absorb specific type(s) of DMG.
ba.shield.name: Shield

ba.spellburst.desc: <@ba.key>Arts Bursts</> are triggered by stacking <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Inflictions</> of the same elements. They include <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>, <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>, <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>, and <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>.
ba.spellburst.name: Arts Burst

item.originiumPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.originiumPowder.name: Originium Powder

ba.spellenhance.desc: Arts Amped targets temporarily deal more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.spellenhance.name: Arts Amp

ba.spellinflict.desc:
<@ba.key>Arts Inflictions</> are special Arts debuff inflicted upon the enemy. There are 4 elements of Arts Inflictions: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>, and <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.key>Arts Infliction</> of the same element can be applied.
ba.spellinflict.name: Arts Infliction

ba.spellinflictonchar.desc:
Some enemy skills can apply stacks of <#ba.burningonchar>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Cryo Infliction</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Nature Infliction</>.
At 4 stacks of the Arts Inflictions, the operator becomes <#ba.burningonchar>Combusted</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electrified</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Solidified</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Corroded</> respectively.
ba.spellinflictonchar.name: Arts Inflictions / Arts Reactions on Operators

ba.vulnerable.desc: Susceptible targets temporarily take more DMG of the element it is Susceptible to. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.vulnerable.name: Susceptible

ba.weak.desc: Weakened targets temporarily deal reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. Effects of the same type can stack non-linearly.
ba.weak.name: Weaken

item.bbflower.desc:
A plant that contains flammable and explosive substances. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
item.bbflower.name: Aketine

item.carbonMtl.desc:
A material produced by refining <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Carbon is essential for crafting <#sys.manualCraftField>planting</> plots, and can also be used for producing other materials.
item.carbonMtl.name: Carbon

item.plantMoss1.desc:
The Buckflower is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its brightly colored flowers.
The plant can also be grown with AIC Factory facilities or <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
The plant can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</> or other materials.
item.plantMoss1.name: Buckflower

item.crystalShell.desc:
A crystalline crust produced by refining <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.crystalShell.name: Origocrust

item.ironEnrPowder.desc:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.ironPowder>Ferrium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironEnrPowder.name: Dense Ferrium Powder

item.ironNugget.desc: A metal ingot produced by refining <#item.ironOre>Ferrium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironNugget.name: Ferrium

item.ironOre.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Ferrium Ore mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</>.
item.ironOre.name: Ferrium Ore

item.originium.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Originium Ore mining spots in the wilderness. It can be used for power generation in the AIC Factory.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.crystalShell>Origocrust</>.
item.originium.name: Originium Ore

item.plantMossPowder3.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials, usually by grinding.
item.plantMossPowder3.name: Sandleaf Powder

item.plantSp1.desc:
The Reed Rye is a type of plantable crop gatherable from the wilderness. It can also be acquired by cultivating <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
It can be directly used for crafting <#sys.manualCraftDish>Fine Food & Meds</> (Fine F&M).
item.plantSp1.name: Reed Rye

item.portSoilMoss1.desc:
Buckflower Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot has a higher chance of getting bonus <#item.plantMossSpc1>Firebuckle</>.
item.portSoilMoss1.name: Buckflower Plot

item.portSoilMoss3.desc:
Sandleaf Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaf</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed3>Sandleaf Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Sandleaves.
item.portSoilMoss3.name: Sandleaf Plot

item.portSoilSp1.desc:
Reed Rye Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantSpSeed1>Reed Rye Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Reed Rye.
item.portSoilSp1.name: Reed Rye Plot

item.quartzPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.quartzPowder.name: Amethyst Powder

sys.tacticalItem.desc:
Tacticals are items that can be used during combat to provide various benefits and buffs. Every tactical has different activation criteria and usage limits.
Every operator can be equipped with 1 tactical. During battle, whenever the tactical activation criteria are met, the operator will auto-use their equipped tactical.
After expending all tactical usage attempts in a single battle, one more use will be restored after a while.
sys.tacticalItem.name: Tacticals

item.procBomb.desc:
Industrial Explosives are a type of explosive that can be produced.
They are made by using the Packaging Unit, an AIC Factory facility. The materials required are <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
Use Industrial Explosives to deal DMG to enemies or take down destructible objects within the blast radius.
item.procBomb.name: Industrial Explosive

item.quartzSand.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Amethyst mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</>.
item.quartzSand.name: Amethyst Ore

sys.manualCraftTonic.desc:
Emergency Food & Meds (EMS F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
They can be acquired by <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> or by using AIC Factory facilities.
sys.manualCraftTonic.name: Emergency Food & Meds
